title: Treat Williams
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Otheruses|Williams}} {{Person |image=[[File:Treat.jpg]] |name=Richard Treat Williams |birth=[[December 1]], [[1950s#1951|1951]]
[[Wikipedia:Rowayton|Rowayton]], [[Wikipedia:Connecticut|Connecticut]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-news/treat-williams-dead-everwood-prince-of-the-city-1235513850|text=Treat Williams, Star of 'Everwood' and 'Prince of the City,' Dies at 71|author=Mike Barnes|work=[[Wikipedia:The Hollywood Reporter|''The Hollywood Reporter'']]|archivedate=20230613023130}} |death=[[June 12]], [[2023]]
[[Wikipedia:Albany, New York|Albany]], [[Wikipedia:New York (state)|New York]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/06/12/obituaries/treat-williams-dead.html|text=Treat Williams, Actor Known for Film Version of 'Hair,' Dies at 71|author=Jesus Jiménez; Mike Ives|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|''The New York Times'']]|archivedate=20230613034629}} |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:Americans|American]] |occupation=*[[Wikipedia:Actor|Actor]] *[[Wikipedia:Film director|Director]] *[[Wikipedia:Film producer|Producer]] |companies= |website= }} {{Quote|Well, Treat Williams decided he was going to come and hang out on the set, which he did, and he appeared in those ice planet scenes as one of the lieutenants or officers in the Rebel Forces. And I'm sure that there is no credit for him at all and he didn't have a character or anything, but he just said, 'Hey, this looks like a lotta fun and I'd kinda like to be a part of it, mind if I take a role of an extra?' Everybody said, 'Yeah!' So here you have this Hollywood star who just decided, for probably no salary, just to hang out for a few scenes and was quite happy with the role of an extra in ''Empire Strikes Back''. And that was kinda the way it was.|[[John Morton]]|{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/jedicouncil/interview/dak.asp|text=Interviews | Cellblock 1138 | John Morton|work=[[TheForce.net]]|archivedate=20041212113203}}}} '''Richard Treat Williams''' ([[December 1]], [[1950s#1951|1951]] – [[June 12]], [[2023]]) was an [[Wikipedia:Americans|American]] screen [[Wikipedia:Actor|actor]]. He was nominated for two [[Wikipedia:Screen Actors Guild Award|Screen Actors Guild Awards]], one [[Wikipedia:Emmy Award|Emmy Award]], and three [[Wikipedia:Golden Globe Award|Golden Globe Awards]]. ==Biography== [[File:Treat_Williams.jpg|thumb|left|225px|Treat Williams and Carrie Fisher pose for photographs on the Echo Base set.]] Treat Williams visited the exclusive set at [[Wikipedia:England|England's]] [[Elstree Studios]] during the [[1979]] filming of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']],{{BookCite|author=Duke, Brad|container=Devil of a Time|title=Harrison Ford: The Films|publisher=[[Wikipedia:McFarland & Company|McFarland & Company, Inc.]]|date=[[2005]]|isbn=978-0786440481}} to visit [[Wikipedia:Actor|actress]] [[Carrie Fisher]] ([[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]]), according to the now-defunct [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature "[[Photoreceptor (Hyperspace)|Photoreceptor]]."{{Hyperspace|url=photoreceptor/2004/11/photoreceptor20041103.html|text=Photoreceptor: "In For a Treat"|int=Photoreceptor (Hyperspace)}} According to actor [[John Morton]] ([[Dak Ralter]]), Williams, who was in [[Wikipedia:London|London]] at the time, decided that it would be fun to appear in the film as an extra, and did so with the blessing of the film's production staff. Donning the tan fatigues of a [[Rebel trooper|Rebel soldier]], Williams appears in the film at some point during the interior [[Echo Base]] scenes on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], according to Morton. During his stay on the set, Williams befriended actor [[Harrison Ford]] ([[Han Solo]]) and posed for photographs with Fisher.''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]'' Williams was uncredited for his role in the film.{{Film|V}} Very few official [[Lucasfilm]] products have addressed the matter of Williams' presence on ''The Empire Strikes Back'' set, and none have definitively stated that the actor actually appeared in the film. To date, only the now-defunct StarWars.com Hyperspace feature "Photoreceptor" ([[2004]]) and the [[2010]] behind-the-scenes book ''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]'', written by author [[Jonathan W. Rinzler]], have officially released black-and-white photographs of Williams, dressed as a Rebel soldier, posing with Carrie Fisher on the Echo Base set, with accompanying captions confirming Williams' identity. Williams died on June 12, 2023, at the age of 71, from injuries sustained in a motorcycle accident. ==Sources== *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/photoreceptor/2004/11/photoreceptor20041101.html|series=Photoreceptor|text=Payback for Artoo}} *''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]'' *[[Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga – The Official Collector's Edition|''Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga'' – The Official Collector's Edition]] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *{{WP|Treat Williams}} *{{IMDb|name/nm0001852}} [[Category:Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back actors]] [[Category:Deceased actors]]
